item.text:
The leak was in `allow()`: the old code computed `recent = [...]` but only used it for `len(recent)`. It never wrote that filtered list back to `self.calls`, so expired timestamps stayed in memory forever while new allowed calls kept appending.

The minimal fix is already present in the working tree at [demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-clean-test/demo/rate_limiter.py:15):

```python
def allow(self) -> bool:
    now = time.time()
    self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
    if len(self.calls) < self.limit:
        self.calls.append(now)
        return True
    return False
```

So the key change is replacing the temporary `recent` variable with assignment back to `self.calls`.

Verification: `python3 -m unittest tests/test_rate_limiter.py` passes.
